classification.summary: BrightSpeed DAP (Dealer/Agent Partner) channel order transaction data tracking orders through issued, installed, canceled, and disconnected lifecycle stages

rules.description: Orders are attributed through a three-level hierarchy: Channel (DAP) → SalesPartner (e.g., INFINITY, CLEARLINK, CENTERFIELD, RED VENTURES, UPDATER, Commission Junction) → SubAgent (tier-specific sub-agents). This implies a multi-party credit/split structure where commissions may flow from BrightSpeed to SalesPartner to SubAgent to individual Agent.

rawSnapshot.evidence: Channel=DAP, PartnerID=BS000208, SalesPartner=INFINITY, SubAgent=INFINITY TIER 3, SalesID=20000303, AgentID=20311

rules.description: Orders are geographically assigned to WireCenters (identified by WireCenterID and WireCenterName) across multiple US states (AL, AR, GA, IL, IN, KS, LA, MI, MO, NC, OH, PA, SC, TN, TX, VA, WI). WireCenters serve as the territory unit linking customer addresses to service areas and sales partner coverage.

Orders must reach COMPLETED status and appear on the Installed sheet to be eligible for commission credit. The Installed sheet includes 'PastDueDate' and 'PastOrigDueDate' Y/N flags that may gate eligibility or trigger different payout rules based on whether installation was late.

past_due_flags.PastDueDate: Y/N flag indicating if install occurred after due date
past_due_flags.PastOrigDueDate: Y/N flag indicating if install occurred after original due date

rawSnapshot.evidence: OrderStatus=COMPLETED, OrderType=NEW INSTALL, PastDueDate=Y, PastOrigDueDate=Y (AL1100040368); PastDueDate=N, PastOrigDueDate=N (MO1100075440)

rules.description: Products are classified by Technology (Fiber vs Copper) and speed tier, which likely maps to different commission rate levels. Observed product tiers include Fiber (100mb, 200mb, 500mb, 600mb, 940mb/1gb, 2gb, 8gb) and Copper (1.5mb, 3mb, 6mb, 10mb, 15mb, 20mb, 30mb, 40mb, 60mb, 80mb), plus value-added services (WiFi Extender, WiFi Protection/Total Home Internet Controls, Voice Line, Inside Wire Protection).

parameters.commission_rates: NOT PRESENT IN FILE - requires separate rate card

rawSnapshot.evidence: Technology=Fiber, ProductName=FIBER INTERNET 1 GIG, Product=Fiber 1gb, Download Speed=1000000; Technology=Copper, ProductName=INTERNET 15 MBPS, Product=Copper 15mb, Download Speed=15000

rules.description: The Disconnected sheet tracks service disconnections linked back to original installation orders (via InstallOrderKey/InstallOrderID) and records the OriginalInstallDate alongside the DisconnectDate. This structure supports a clawback mechanism where commissions paid on installations may be recovered if the customer disconnects within a certain window. Observed disconnect-to-install intervals range from days (same-month rapid churn) to ~5 months.

tracking_fields.InstallOrderKey: Links back to the original install order

parameters.clawback_window: NOT SPECIFIED IN FILE - requires comp plan document

rawSnapshot.evidence: DisconnectDate=01/01/2026, OriginalInstallDate=12/31/2025, InstallOrderKey=NC1100151468_1 (rapid churn); DisconnectDate=01/01/2026, OriginalInstallDate=07/30/2025, InstallOrderKey=AL1100029167_1 (5-month tenure)

rules.description: The Canceled sheet includes a ConsolidatedReason field categorizing why orders were canceled. Certain reasons may affect commission eligibility differently (e.g., 'Duplicate Order' or 'Account Cleanup' vs. 'Buyer's Remorse'). Observed cancellation reasons include: Buyer's Remorse, Buyer's Remorse - Order > 30 Days, Buyer's Remorse - Order > 45 Days, Cancel/Reissue, Account Cleanup, Cancelled per Customer, Customer Reqs Reschedule, Duplicate Order, Invalid Order Residential, Missed appointment, No Access/Not Home, Other, Product/Speed not Available.

parameters.commission_impact: NOT SPECIFIED IN FILE - requires comp plan document to determine which cancellation reasons trigger commission reversal

rawSnapshot.evidence: ConsolidatedReason values: Buyer's Remorse, Cancel/Reissue, Other, Account Cleanup, Buyer's Remorse - Order > 45 Days, Duplicate Order, Cancelled per Customer, Customer Reqs Reschedule

rules.description: SubAgent values encode a tier structure (TIER 1, TIER 2, TIER 3, MISC) that likely corresponds to different commission rates or payout levels per sales partner. For example, INFINITY has sub-agents labeled 'INFINITY TIER 1', 'INFINITY TIER 2', 'INFINITY TIER 3', 'INFINITY MISC', 'INFINITY ACQ - DISPLAY', 'INFINITY ACQ - TIER 1'. CLEARLINK has 'CLEARLINK TIER 1', 'CLEARLINK TIER 2', 'CLEARLINK TIER 3' variants with language/market suffixes. Cart-based naming (e.g., 'Infinity Branded Tier 1 Cart', 'ClearLink Branded Tier 2 Cart') suggests online acquisition channels.

parameters.commission_rate_per_tier: NOT SPECIFIED IN FILE - requires rate card

rawSnapshot.evidence: SubAgent values: INFINITY TIER 1, INFINITY TIER 2, INFINITY TIER 3, INFINITY ACQ - DISPLAY, INFINITY MISC, Infinity Branded Tier 2 Cart, ClearLink Branded Tier 2 Cart, CLEARLINK TIER 3 BRANDED - CLR MKT PX

rules.description: Commission eligibility likely varies by OrderType. Observed types are NEW INSTALL (vast majority), MIGRATION (upgrade from existing service), and DISCONNECT. New installs and migrations appear on the Issued/Installed sheets as commissionable events, while disconnects are tracked separately and likely trigger clawback. The Issued sheet named range spans 6,018 rows and Installed spans 4,446 rows, suggesting a significant installation conversion rate.

rawSnapshot.evidence: OrderType=NEW INSTALL (majority), OrderType=MIGRATION (AR1100033105); Named range _xlnm._FilterDatabase=Issued!$A$1:$AC$6018, Installed!$A$1:$AF$4446

insights: This file is transactional deal data, NOT a compensation plan document. It contains no explicit commission rates, quota targets, payout formulas, or plan terms. The data serves as the input feed for a compensation calculation engine. To extract actual compensation rules, the following companion documents are needed: (1) A DAP Partner Rate Card specifying commission amounts per product tier, technology type, and partner tier level; (2) A clawback policy defining the time window and conditions under which commissions on disconnected services are recovered; (3) A cancellation policy defining which ConsolidatedReason codes trigger commission reversal vs. no-pay; (4) Quota/target definitions per partner or agent if accelerators/decelerators apply; (5) The partner agreement defining the split structure between BrightSpeed, SalesPartner, SubAgent, and individual Agent. The tier structure embedded in SubAgent names (Tier 1/2/3) strongly suggests a tiered rate table exists elsewhere. The PastDueDate/PastOrigDueDate flags on the Installed sheet likely tie to an SLA-based qualifier or penalty. The presence of both BS Broadband and QF Broadband brands suggests potential rate differentiation by service brand.
